def: s.m. Name of a wild mountain race (who live in the Vindhya mountains, in the forests of Malwa, Mewar, and Kandesh, and subsist chiefly by plunder).
s.f. The seed of the marking-nut plant (see bhilāwāṅ).

s.m. The marking-nut plant, Semecarpus anacardium (commonly called 'the Malacca bean'); the Acājon or cashew nut (from which is extracted an acid juice used for medicinal purposes, as well as a black liquid used for marking linen. It is also used for making wales on the body, which are shown in a criminal court as evidence of having been beaten).

s.m. The marking-nut plant, Semecarpus anacardium (commonly called 'the Malacca bean'); the Acājon or cashew nut (from which is extracted an acid juice used for medicinal purposes, as well as a black liquid used for marking linen. It is also used for making wales on the body, which are shown in a criminal court as evidence of having been beaten).
s.m. = bhīl, q.v.; — barbarian, ignorant fellow; fool.
s.m. A bhīl, q.v.; barbarian, &c. = bhil, q.v.
s.m. See bhillā and bhīl.
s.f. A woman or girl of the bhīl race (see bhil; bhillā; bhīl).
s.f. A woman or girl of the Bhīl race; the wife of a Bhīl.
adj. = bhīru, q.v.
adj. = bhīruk, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Fearful, terrible, terrific, horrible, dreadful, frightful, tremendous; — horror, terror; danger; — name of the second of the five Pānḍū princes, the second brother of Yudhishṭhir; an epithet of Śiva
subs: s.f. lit. 'The terrible night'; the seventh night of the seventh month in the seventy-seventh year of a man's life (supposed to be the ordinary period of human life); extreme old age, dotage
s.m. lit. Having a terrible army'; name of the second son of Panḍu ( = bhīm)

def: s.f. Name of a form of Durgā, the wife of Śiva; a whip.
s.m. The great crested Drongo shrike (brought from Nepal and the Bhotan Doars); the mocking-bird of India, Edolius malabaricus.
adj. Wet, moist ( = bhīngā); light (colour, or smell).
s.m. Sister's husband ( = bahneʼū, q.v.).
v.n. To sound, give forth a ringing sound, to ring; to buzz (as a fly), hum (as a bee, &c.); to be covered with flies, to swarm; to look, or to be, dirty or filthy; to sit idle, have nothing to do.
s.m. One who speaks through the nose; &c. (see next).
s.f. Buzzing; buzz, hum ( = bhin-bhin, q.v.).
s.f. = H بهينچنا भींचना bhīṅćnā, v.t. = bhīć; bhīćnā; qq.v.
v.t. = H بهينچ भींच bhīṅć, s.f. = bhīć; bhīćnā; qq.v.
s.m. Round ball or mass; lump, mass, block; lump or ball of tobacco: bhinḍe-ḵẖāna, s.m. The room appropriated to the apparatus of the ḥuqqa.
s.m. Round ball or mass; lump, mass, block; lump or ball of tobacco: bhinḍe-ḵẖāna, s.m. The room appropriated to the apparatus of the ḥuqqa.
s.f. The vegetable Hibiscus abelmoschus, or H. esculentus (commonly called, in India, 'lady's finger').
s.m. A short dart or arrow thrown from the hand or shot through a tube; a stone fastened to a string.
part. adj. = bhīngā; bhīgā; qq.v.
part. adj. = bhīgā, q.v.
v.t. = bhigānā, q.v.
v.n. To be wet, &c. = bhīgnā, q.v.

part. adj. & s.m. Broken, split, divided, disunited; scattered, separated; separate, apart, distinct, diverse; distinguished; other, different; opened, unfolded, expanded, blown; — (in Arith.), a fraction (syn. kasr)
subs: (bhinna+abhinna), adj. Separate and not separate, distinct and not distinct
adj. & adv. Various, diverse, separate, different; — separately, severally, one by one
s.m. One not belonging to the same family or tribe, one of a different lineage
s.m. (in Arith.), Cube of a fraction
s.m. (in Arith.) Square of a fraction
adj. & s.m. Of different colour; of a different tribe or caste; — a man of a different caste
(bhinna+udara), s.m. Brother not by the same mother, half-brother

def: s.m. Separatist, seceder, heterodox sectary; Buddhist; Jain; — name of a musical mode.
s.f. Separateness; separation, distinction, difference.
s.m. See s.v. bhinna.
s.m. Dawn of day, day-break, day-light, early morning (also written bhinusār, bhinnsār); — adv. At dawn, &c.
s.f. = bhīt, q.v.
s.m. Wasp = bhiṛ, q.v.
s.m. Corr. of bīr, q.v.
s.f. Wasp; hornet (syn. bhir, birnī)
subs: lit. 'To put one's hand into a wasp's nest'; to raise a hornet's nest round oneself; to stir up mischief or evil
To rouse a nest of sleeping hornets; to rouse a sleeping foe; to revive an old quarrel.

def: s.m. Hero, &c. = bīr, q.v.; also = bhīṛ, q.v.
s.f. Press, crush; multitude, crowd, throng, concourse; press of work, trouble, difficulty
subs: v.n. To be overwhelmed with difficulties, troubles, or misfortunes
bhīṛ lagānā, v.n. To crowd, throng, swarm.

def: part. adj. Pressed, crushed, crowded, thronged, packed close; narrow, close, tight, contracted; — s.m. One who has contracted eye-brows.
adj. (f. -ī), Joined, touching, in contact; closed, shut; ajar; narrow, close; crowded, cramped; in collision, at loggerheads
s.f. Contact; closing (together, or upon one another); collision; shock (of armies).
s.m. Contentious, quarrelsome, or pugnacious person.
s.m. Wolf ( = bheṛiyā)
s.f. A species of falcon = bahrī, q.v.
v.n. To be open or ajar (a door: — cf. bhiṛnā).

v.n. To draw near, approach, come together, close to (as shutters, &c.); to be placed in contact, be joined; to come in contact (with), to close (as two armies, &c.), come into collision, to clash, slam, bang (as a door, &c.); to join battle, to contend, fight; to twine the arms (round, -se), to clasp, embrace.

v.t. To bring together, place in close contact, cause to touch or be joined, to join; to close (a door, &c.); to bring into collision, cause to fight; — to bribe; to stake: bhiṛā-denā, v.t. = bhiṛānā.
s.f. Dispersion, flight.
s.m.f. See bhīrū.
s.m.f. Fearful, timid, cowardly; shy; — coward; shy or timid person; jackal; tiger; goat; centipede; a sort of prickly nightshade, Solanum Jacquini; the plant Asparagus racemosus.
adj. & s.m. = bhīru, q.v.
s.m. (?), The edible root of the lotus (cf. basenḍ).
s.m. The edible root of the lotus ( = bhis).
v.n. To be dazzled (the sight: cf. phisalnā).
s.f. Fear, dread, terror, fright; intimidation.
s.m. A physician.
adj. & s.m. Horrible, terrific, formidable, awful; — the sentiment of horror; the property that excites fear; horror, terror; object of horror, &c.; — the olibanum tree, Boswellia thurifera.
adj. & s.m. Terrible, horrible, terrific, fearful; — horror, the sentiment of horror; goblin, imp, rākshas; name of the granduncle of the Pānḍus, and son of the Ganges; name of Śiva
subs: (bhīshma+ashṭamī), s.f. The eighth day in the light half of the month Māgh (when there is a festiva sacred to Bhīshma)
s.f. The mother of Bhīshma, the Ganges.

def: s.f. The feces, excrement; filth.
s.f. Unclean, or dirty-looking woman; slattern; foolish or stupid woman.
s.f. = bhīt, q.v.
s.m. Small hut, hovel; hole, lair, or den (of a wild animal); pig-sty (cf. bhaṭ).
part. adj. Frightened; fearful, timid, afraid.
s.m. Old house, former residence (provided some vestige remains).
v.n. To come in, to enter
subs: bhītarā-bāhirā, adv. Half in half out
v.n. To be inside or within; to be in, be contained, included, or comprehended.

def: adj. Interior, internal, inward, inner, inside; hidden, secret

s.m.f. The people who live in a house or family (opp. to bāhariyā, 'outsiders'), inmates; domestic; one admitted to the inner apartments of a house; guest at a marriage feast (who eats in company with the relations of the bride); — one who betrays a secret.
s.m. Ground rent paid for the site of a house (by a stranger).
s.f. Fear, apprehension, alarm; trembling.
s.f. = bhīt, q.v.
s.f. Wall; breadth of a wall; embankment; vestige of an old house.

ety: S वृत्, or अभ्यावृत्; see bheṭ
def: v.n. To come in contact (with, -se), be touched (by); to be defiled or polluted by contact (with).
s.f. Nipple (of a woman's breast).
s.f. Wall ( = bhīt, q.v.); breaking; a thing broken or divided; fragment; figure; defect; place, spot; opportunity, occasion; asylum.
s.f. Wall ( = bhīt, q.v.); small house lizard.
s.f. Fear, apprehension, dread ( = bhay).
s.f. Coral.
s.m. = bhoṛal, q.v.

s.m. Enjoyment, fruition; eating; use, application; usufruct, use of a deposit; utility, advantage, profitable aim or object; possessing, possession; cherishing, nourishing, protecting; suffering, experiencing; enduring, feeling, perception (of joy or sorrow), pleasure, satisfaction, gratification; suffering, passion; requital; abuse, rudeness, insult; any object of enjoyment, that which is eaten, food, victuals, repast; sexual enjoyment, carnal intercourse; dressed food offered to an idol; morning hymns, matins; — gain, profit, produce; money, wealth; hire, hire of dancing-girls or courtezans; (in Arith.) numerator of a fraction
subs: (bhoga+adhikāra), s.m. Possession of the usufruct of mortgaged or pledged property; — bhog-ādhikārī, s.m. Possessor of the usufruct of mortgaged or pledged property
(bhoga+vilāsa), s.m. Luxurious living; sensual pleasure, amorous enjoyment, sexual intercourse; — bhog-bilāsī, s.m. One who is addicted to luxuriousness, or to sensual pleasure, voluptuary
s.m. Mortgage with possession of the property by the mortgagee and of the usufruct thereof; bond granting the use of a pledged article
s.m. Person in actual possession of anything; possessor; lord, governor
s.m. Possessor or enjoyer of any property
bhog sunānā, v.t. To abuse, vilify
adj. & s.m. Producing or affording enjoyment; procuring food or pleasure; — one who affords enjoyment
v.t. To experience, suffer, undergo; to enjoy; to be requited for; to have or enjoy sexual intercourse with
bhog lagānā or lagā-denā or laga-lenā
v.t. To eat, partake of food; to place food before (one); to offer food to an idol
(fem. of next), s.f. A woman who yields enjoyment
adj. & s.m. Furnished with enjoyment, yielding enjoyment, delightful, pleasurable, enjoyable; — one who yields enjoyment.

def: s.m. Snake; expanded hood of a snake; the body; particular array of troops, army in column
subs: (fem. of next), s.f. Name of the capital of the serpent race; name of the Ganges of the lower regions
adj. & s.m. Furnished with windings or curves; — a snake.

def: s.m. Deception, imposition, illusion, deceit, fraud, trick, treachery, roguery, cheating.
s.m. Enjoying, &c. (see bhognā)
s.f. = bhog-vatī, q.v.s.v. bhog.
s.f. A small basket in which the sower carries his seed.

adj. & s.m. Enjoying; using, possessing; suffering, experiencing; full of enjoyments; devoted to enjoyments, jovial, jolly; indulging in sensual pleasures; — one who enjoys or possesses (a thing, &c.), one who makes mere pleasure a pursuit, man of pleasure, one given up to carnal pleasures, voluptuary, rake, debauchee; — king, prince; head-man of a village; barber.
adj. & s.m. Having windings or curves, curved; — a snake.
s.f. Female enjoyer; royal concubine.
s.m. Voluptuary, &c. = bhogī, q.v.
v.t. To enjoy; to experience, suffer, undergo; to be requited for, to taste; to endure, bear, put up with; to take or receive pleasure or pain with indifference, to live stoically.
s.m. Grant of revenue for the use or enjoyment of an individual (especially of a Brāhmaṇ or religious person).
s.m. = bhokta; bhogī, qq.v.
part. adj. & s.m. Enjoyable, to be enjoyed, to be used; to be endured or experienced; — what may be enjoyed, anything that may be possessed or used, an object of fruition, possession, wealth.
s.m. Eating, enjoying, feasting; meal; feast, banquet.
s.m. A species of birch
subs: bhoj-pattar, s.m. The leaf and the bark of the bhoj tree (used for writing on, and also in making tubes and pipes for the ḥuqqa).

def: s.m. Name of the country of Patna and Bhāgalpur; name of a celebrated sovereign of Malwa (supposed to have flourished about the end of the tenth century or beginning of the eleventh, and to have been a great patron of learning); — a cowherd
subs: bhoj-kaṭ, s.m. = bhoj (the country); — bhoj-purī, adj. & s.m. Appertaining to Bhojpur; — an inhabitant of Bhojpur; — s.f. The dialect of Hindī spoken in Bhojpur.

def: s.m. Skilful judge of food
subs: v.n. To eat, partake of food, take a meal: to give food (to, -kā), to feed, give as food.

def: adj. = bhojya, q.v.
part. adj. & s.m. Suitable or fit to be eaten, eatable, edible; — anything eatable or enjoyable, food
s.m. = bhoṅkas, q.v.
v.n. = bhauṅkna, q.v.
v.t. = bhoṅknā, q.v.
s.m. Enjoyer, eater, feeder; good liver; epicure; agent in fruition; one who employs or makes use of; one who feels or experiences (joy, sorrow, &c.); possessor, lord, ruler; husband; the soul.

ety: S. भाव+लः; or prob. Prk. भमरओ; S. भ्रमर+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Natural, simple, artless, undesigning, guileless, harmless, innocent, simple-minded; silly, stupid (cf. S. bhela); — an epithet of Śiva
subs: s.f. Simple or artless woman or girl; silly woman
s.f. Simple or guileless speech; innocent prattle

def: s.m. Simplicity, artlessness, undesigningness, guilelessness, innocence; silliness, stupidity.
s.m. = bholāpan, q.v.
s.f. See s.v. bholā.
s.f. Coral.
s.m. = bhoṅpū, q.v.
s.f. Sound of a wind instrument; deep, bass sound (as of the lowing of a cow, or the bark of a dog, &c.)
s.m. A dung-beetle ( = bhoṅṛā, bhauṅṛā, q.v.).
adj. (f. -ī), Forbidding, monstrous, ugly, frightful, uncouth, ill-shaped, deformed, unsightly; bad, unlucky, inauspicious
subs: adj. Of unlucky footsteps; unlucky, ill-omened.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Forbidding, monstrous, ugly, frightful, uncouth, ill-shaped, deformed, unsightly; bad, unlucky, inauspicious
subs: adj. Of unlucky footsteps; unlucky, ill-omened.

def: adj. & s.m. Mild, meek, soft, simple, artless, innocent; silly, stupid, loutish; — a soft, a booby, noodle, lout, green-horn, stupid fellow (syn. modhū).
s.m. Prick, thrust, stab, stick
s.m. Prick, thrust, stab, stick

ety: S. भयानकस्, from भय, rt. भी
def: s.m. A wizard who preys upon human beings till he brings them to the grave; wizard, sorcerer, magician.
v.t. To thrust, drive in, pierce, prick, stab, stick; to make big holes in sewing, sew badly.
adj. Very large and fat.

r: bhoṅp, bhauṅp, bhoṅpā, bhauṅpā, bhoṅpū, bhauṅpū, bhoṅ
def: s.m. A kind of wind instrument; a horn; (syn. narsinghā.

r: bhoṅpā, bhauṅpā, bhoṅp, bhauṅp, bhoṅpū, bhauṅpū, bhoṅ
def: s.m. A kind of wind instrument; a horn; (syn. narsinghā.

r: bhoṅpū, bhauṅpū, bhoṅp, bhauṅp, bhoṅpā, bhauṅpā, bhoṅ
def: s.m. A kind of wind instrument; a horn; (syn. narsinghā.
adj. (f. -ī), Blunt, dull, obtuse; weak, feeble; inferior, of less worth ( = bhutrā).
adj. (f. -ī), Blunt, dull, obtuse; weak, feeble; inferior, of less worth ( = bhutrā).
adj. (f. -ī), Blunt, dull, obtuse; weak, feeble; inferior, of less worth ( = bhutrā).
s.m. A kind of jogī, or faqīr, a magician.
s.f. Break of day, dawn, daylight, early morning
subs: v.t. To let daylight into (one), give (one) a good beating
adv. At day-break, at dawn, in the morning
v.n. To be morn; to be finished or terminated; to be in distress, to suffer much, be in trouble.

def: adj. (f. -ī) = bholā, q.v.
adj. & s.f. = bholī, q.v.
s.m. = bhosṛā, q.v.
s.f. A term of abuse contemptuously applied to a woman.
s.f. A term of abuse contemptuously applied to a woman.
s.m. The country of Bhoṭān; cloth or clothing from the Bhoṭ country.
adj. Blunt, &c. = bhoṅthā, bhoṅthrā, q.v.
s.m. The country of Bhoṭān ( = bhoṭ).
s.m. The country of Bhoṭān ( = bhoṭ).
s.f. Labour, toil, work.
s.m. Labourer.
s.m. A native of Bhoṭ.
s.f. The wife of a bhoʼī, &c. (see bhoʼī).
s.f. The wife of a bhoʼī, &c. (see bhoʼī).
adj. (f. -ī), Shining, gleaming, glittering, brilliant, splendid.

s.m. Moving round, whirling; roaming, wandering, erring, straying; deviation, aberration; perplexity, bewilderment, confusion; maze, labyrinth; error, misapprehension, blunder, mistake, slip; doubt, suspicion; apprehension, supposition, presumption; — whirlwind; whirlpool; lathe; potter's wheel
subs: v.t. To doubt, suspect, question; — v.n. To be vain, proud, puffed up, have one's head turned
v.n. To be disclosed (a secret); to be exposed (pretensions).

def: adj. & s.m. Causing to whirl; causing error or mistake, bewildering, perplexing, puzzling, misleading, deceptive, deceitful; — deceiver, inveigler, cheat, rogue, swindler; a jackal; a sort of loadstone or magnet (so called from its causing iron to turn round); — the sun-flower, Helianthus annuus.
s.m. Roaming, roving, wandering about; turning round, whirling; turn round, whirl; perambulation; giddiness; falling into error or mistake
v.t. To cause to stray, cause to err, to mislead, misguide, lead astray, beguile, deceive; to allure, tempt; to perplex; to alarm.
s.f. A sort of game played by women for the amusement of husbands or lovers.
s.m. Whirling round; dancing round; vertigo, giddiness, epilepsy; a kind of loadstone; honey; a village.
s.m. A large black bee, the humble bee; — lover, gallant, libertine; — giddiness, vertigo; epilepsy.
adj. Whirling round, revolving; affected with giddiness or vertigo, giddy; having epilepsy, epileptic; made of honey; — s.m. An epileptic.
s.f. Bee; — the plant Jatuka; the plant Putra-dātrī.
part. adj. Going round, whirling about, revolving; roaming, roving, wandering, erring.

s.f. Roaming or wandering about; whirling round; wandering; giddiness, dizziness; the being in error or mistake, erroneousness; suspiciousness, suspicion, doubt; unsteadiness, vacillation; — worldly lust, sensual desire.
s.f. Whirling or turning round, turning about, going round, circulating, revolving; whirlwind, &c.; forgetfulness; error, slip, &c. = bhram, q.v.
adj. Whirling round, &c. ( = bhramat, q.v.); suspicious, doubting, sceptical; vain, proud, self-sufficient; ostentatious, showy.
v.n. To roam, wander, stray, err.
adj. (f. -ī), Mixed with error.
s.m. Falling (from), falling off, declining (from a height or from propriety), fall (from virtue); abandoning, dropping, desertion; overthrow, destruction, ruin.
s.m. Wanderer, one who has gone astray.
part. Whirled, revolved; erred, gone astray, fallen into error, mistaken; wandering, erring.
s.f. Whirling round; wandering, going astray; error, mistake, blunder; ignorance.
s.f. = bhrānti, q.v.
adj. Erring, &c. = bhrānta, q.v.
part. adj. Fallen, degraded, debased, polluted, abominable, depraved, lost, vicious, dissolute; outraged, violated, debauched; impure, unclean, foul, corrupt
subs: adj. Fallen from, or deprived of, a kingdom; deposed
v.t. To corrupt, pervert, debase, pollute, defile; to violate, seduce, ravish
v.n. To be fallen, be debased, polluted, &c.; to calcine.

def: adj. & s.f. Fallen, gone astray, defiled, violated (a woman); — fallen female, unchaste woman, adulteress, harlot
subs: v.t. To lead astray, seduce, defile, pollute.

def: s.m. Fallen condition, degradation; depravity, corruption, dissoluteness, &c.
s.m. Fallen condition, degradation; depravity, corruption, dissoluteness, &c.
s.m. Brother = bhāʼī, q.v.
s.m. Brother = bhāʼī, q.v.
adj. Pertaining to a brother, brotherly, fraternal; — bhrātrīya, s.m. Brother's son, nephew.
adj. Pertaining to a brother, brotherly, fraternal; — bhrātrīya, s.m. Brother's son, nephew.

s.m. Table land, level summit of a mountain; declivity, slope, cliff, precipice; — name of one of the ten Mahārshis or primeval sages: name of Jamadagni; also of Śiva; name of the descendants of Bhr̤igu
subs: s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts descended from Bhr̤igu
s.m. The mark made by Kr̤ishṇa on his own breast to shew the place where Bhr̤igu kicked him

def: s.m. A male actor or dancer in female attire. Written also bhr̤ikunś, &c
s.f. The eye-brow; contraction of the brow, angry look, frown, scowl.
s.m. A large black bee ( = bhramar); the fork-tailed shrike ( = bhujangā, and bhaṅg-rāj); — a golden vase; a libertine; woody cassia; the plant Verbesina prostrata
subs: bhr̤ing-rajāh̥, s.m. The shrike ( = bhr̤ing); the medicinal plant Eclipta prostrata, or Verbesina prostrata, or V. scandens, or V. calendulacea.

def: s.m. A species of shrike, Lanius malabaricus.
s.m. A kind of golden vase (used at royal ceremonials); gold; cloves; the plant Verbesina calendulacea.
s.f. A cricket.
s.f. A species of large black bee ( = bhr̤ing); a species of wasp (either the common kind or the Vespa solitaria; — syn. bhiṛ)
part. adj. Roasted, baked, fried, grilled, parched, scorched.
s.m. Hireling, hired servant or labourer, mercenary; — adj. (used at the end of comp.), Bearing, supporting, carrying, nourishing.
s.f. Service for wages; nourishment, support, maintenance, sustenance, food; wages, hire; capital, principal.
s.f. Maintenance, support; hire, wages.
s.m. Dependent, servant, slave (see bhr̤it).
s.m. Service, servitude, dependence, slavery.
s.f. Eyebrow (syn. bhauṅ)
subs: s.m. Contraction of the eyebrows, angry look, frown
s.f. = bhrū; and bhrū-bhaṅg (also written bhr̤ikuṭī, and bhrakuṭī)
s.m. Actor or dancer in female attire
s.f. The eyebrow compared to the gentle bends of a twining plant.

def: s.m. Embryo, fœtus; unborn child; pregnancy
subs: bhrūṇa-hā, bhrūṇa-han, adj. & s.m. Fœtus-killing; — one who occasions or procures abortion
s.f. Murder of the fœtus; causing or procuring abortion.

def: s.f. The earth, world; land, ground, soil; site, place (syn. bhūmi); sacrificial fire; (in Geom.), the base of a plane figure
subs: s.m. The burden of the world (as sustained by Vishnu in his incarnate state)
bhū-pālak, s.m. lit. 'Earth-guardian'; sovereign, king, prince; landlord
s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode
bhūp, s.m. = bhū-pāl; — bhū-pa-bimān, s.m. Carriage of a king; — bhūpāvamān (bhū-pa+ava+māna), s.m. Disrespect of a monarch
s.m. The surface of the earth
s.m. The earth, the ground, face of the earth
s.f. Wheat; the plant Flacourtia sapida, or the fruit of this plant
s.m. 'Earth-animal'; a kind of snail; earth-worm
s.f. Motion or course of the earth; earthquake ( = bhū-kamp)
adj. & s.m. Moving on land, or on the ground; — any land animal
s.m. 'Earth circle'; the equator or equinoctial line; rotation of the earth
s.m. Grant of land, or of the revenue thereof; — bhū-dān-patra, s.m. Deed of a grant of land
s.m. Mountain; a kind of chemical or medical apparatus, a sort of sand-bath (in which a covered crucible is placed, the fire being lighted both above and below it)
s.m. A divinity upon earth, a Brāhman
s.f. A sort of sunflower, Heliotropium indicum: bhū-swāmī, s.m. Landlord, landholder
s.m. lit. 'Heaven on earth'; an epithet of the mountain Sumeru
s.m. lit. 'Earth-ball'; the terrestrial globe, the earth; — bhū-gol vidyā, s.f. Knowledge of the terrestrial globe; the science of geography; — bhūgol-kī bast vidya, s.f. Physical geography
s.m. The terrestrial world, the earth, the habitation of mortals
adj. Situated in the middle of the earth, or in the centre of the world; — bhū-madhyastha-sāgar, s.m. The Mediterranean sea
s.m. The terrestrial globe, the earth
adj. Made, or formed, or consisting of earth, produced from the earth, earthen, earthy, terrene
s.f. An epithet of Ćhāyā or Shadow (personified as wife of the sun)
s.m. A kind of snail or earth-worm; the serpent which is supposed to support the earth
bhū-nīmb, s.m. The plant Gentiana cherayta (com. called ćiraita)

def: s.f. Father's sister, paternal aunt ( = phūʼā; būʼā).
s.m. Worm; caterpillar.
v.n. To become sticky, &c. ( = ubasnā)
s.m. Snake = bhujang, q.v.
s.m. Hot ashes, embers.
s.m. Hot ashes, embers.
adj. Hungry, &c. = bhūkhā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Rude, uncivilized, ignorant, barbarous; — barbarian, clown, lout, blockhead, ignorant or stupid fellow.
s.m. = bhūṅ-ćampa, q.v.s.v. bhūṅ.
s.m. = bhujaṅg, bhujaṅgā, q.v.
s.m. Light, sandy soil (cf. bhūṛ).
s.m. = bhūbhal, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Fit to enjoy; fit to suffer or undergo; deserving punishment; — one who deserves punishment or suffering.
v.t. To enjoy; to experience, realize, suffer, undergo, endure, put up with, pay the penalty of, be requited for; to terminate, accomplish; to settle with, try conclusions with.
adj. & s.m. Simple, foolish; — simpleton, fool, &c. = buggā, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. bhū.

v.t. To cause to enjoy; to cause to experience, cause to suffer or undergo, cause to be realized; to do, perform, execute, discharge; to finish, complete, have done with, dispose of; to settle, adjust, pay off, liquidate; to requite; to distribute, apportion, portion out.
s.m. Chair-porter, pālkī-bearer, bearer (com. called 'boy' by Europeans); the head of a Gouḍ village; — a white cat.
s.f. = bhūṅ; bhūmi; qq.v.; a kind of caterpillar covered with hair, the palmer worm (syn. bhunḍlī)
subs: s.m. Gifts at marriages and funerals (so called from the ceremony of burning earth, which is done previous to the presentation)
s.m. Name of a low caste of Hindūs engaged in the vilest offices
s.f. Land let at a low rent to military retainers. (N.B. Compounds not entered here, such as bhūʼīṅ-ćampā, bhūʼīṅ-ḍol, &c., will be found under the words bhū, bhūṅ, or bhūmi).

def: s.m. An ornament worn on the upper arm, armlet ( = bāzū-band); anything tied on the arm above the elbow
subs: (-se), To clasp in the arms, to embrace
s.m. Ornament for the arm, armlet
bhujā-ḍanḍ, s.m. Arm as hard as a staff, muscular arm
s.m. The upper part of the arm near the shoulder.

def: s.m. An ornament worn on the upper arm, armlet ( = bāzū-band); anything tied on the arm above the elbow
subs: (-se), To clasp in the arms, to embrace
s.m. Ornament for the arm, armlet
bhujā-ḍanḍ, s.m. Arm as hard as a staff, muscular arm
s.m. The upper part of the arm near the shoulder.

def: s.m. Snake = bhujang, q.v.
adj. Black, jet-black (the colour of the shrike: generally used in comp. with kālā, e.g. kālā bhujang, adj. Very black, jet-black)
subs: s.m. A species of metre, four times ̆¯¯ ̆¯¯̆¯¯̆¯¯; — bhujange uṛānā, v.n. To be in great distress and poverty; to spread false reports.

def: adj. Black, jet-black (the colour of the shrike: generally used in comp. with kālā, e.g. kālā bhujang, adj. Very black, jet-black)
subs: s.m. A species of metre, four times ̆¯¯ ̆¯¯̆¯¯̆¯¯; — bhujange uṛānā, v.n. To be in great distress and poverty; to spread false reports.

def: s.m. Snake ( = bhujang).
s.f. A species of metre ( = bhujang-prayāt).
s.f. Fried pot-herbs = bhujiyā, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ā), Independent man
subs: slave; a kind of string worn round the waist.

def: s.f. Fried or cooked pot-herbs, greens ( = bhājī); — parched rice or grain.
s.m. Parched or scorched grain.
s.m. = bhujnā, q.v.
s.m. Hole, cavity, chasm; source of a stream, head of a fountain; darkness; time.
s.f. = bhūkh, q.v.
adj. = bhūkhā, q.v.
s.m. = bhūkhan; bhūshaṇ, q.v.
adj. Dialect. form of bhūkhā, q.v.

ety: S. बुभुक्षा, desid. of rt. भुज्
def: s.f. Desire of eating, appetite, hunger; desire of enjoying anything, craving (for, -kī)
subs: s.f. Hunger and thirst; desire for food, appetite; — bhūkh-piyās mārnā (-kī), To repress (one's) appetite; to mortify (oneself)
(-ko), To feel hunger, be hungry, have an appetite
s.m. Famished or starving person, starveling; greedy person
v.n. Hunger or appetite to pass away.

def: adj. (f. -ī), & s.m. Hungered, hungry, famished, starving, pinched with hunger; greedy, ravenous; desirous (of), craving (for); poor, indigent, destitute; — hungry or starving person; poor man; beggar
subs: bhūkhe marnā, būkhon marnā, v.n. To be in a famishing state, to famish, die of hunger, starve to death.

def: s.m. = bhūshaṇ.
v.n. = bhauṅknā, q.v.
v.n. To be pierced, be pricked.
s.m. Name of a forest tribe of Rājpūts.
part. adj. & s.m. Enjoyed, eaten; used, possessed; experienced, suffered; — act of eating; eater; thing eaten or enjoyed, food.
s.f. Enjoying, enjoyment, eating; food; fruition, possession, usufruct.
s.f. Forgetfulness, oversight, neglect, negligence, omission, failure, miss; slip, mistake, error, blunder, fault
part. adj. Forgetful; erring; lost, astray (used chiefly in comp.)
subs: bhūlā-bhaṭkā, bhūlā-ćūkā, adj. adv. & s.m. Losing or missing the road, wandering, led astray, astray; calling casually, or in consequence of an accident (on another); — by or through forgetfulness, or neglect; &c.; — a casual or chance visitor, one who calls in consequence of some accident, &c. (not intentionally)
s.m. A forgetful or bad memory.

def: adj. & s.m. Forgetful, oblivious; thoughtless, careless, negligent; blundering, stupid; — one who is very forgetful; a great blunderer, &c. (syn. bhullaṛ).
v.t. = bhulānā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to be forgotten; to cause to forget; to cause to lose; to inveigle; to deceive; to mislead, lead astray; to bewilder; to fascinate; to coax, amuse, allure, divert
s.m. Leading into error, misleading, deception, fraud, cheating; deceit; feint, trick, hoax
subs: (-ko), To beguile, deceive; to mislead; to play a trick upon.

def: v.t. = bhulānā, q.v.

ety: fr. S. ज्वालष्यति, cf. the H. jhulasnā; and the H. balnā and jalnā
def: v.n. To be burnt, scorched, singed, charred; v.t. To burn, scorch, &c. (bhulsānā); — to fling or throw a gift (at).
s.f. contrac. of bhulāwaṭ, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Misleading, deceiving; — misleader, deceiver, inveigler.
s.m. Leading into error, misleading, deception, fraud, cheating; deceit; feint, trick, hoax
subs: (-ko), To beguile, deceive; to mislead; to play a trick upon.

def: s.f. Forgetfulness, obliviousness.
adj. & s.m. = bhulakkaṛ, q.v.

ety: bhūl˚ or bhol˚ = Prk. भुल्ल(इ), fr. भुल्ल = भुल्ह = भुड्ढ = S. भ्रष्ट p.p.p. of rt. भ्रंश्
def: v.n. To be forgotten; to be led away, be misled, be deceived (by, -par); to forget, err, go astray, stray, mistake, err, go wrong, blunder; — v.t. To forget; to overlook, omit, miss
v.t. To burn, scorch, singe.
s.m. Burning, scorching, &c.
s.f. = bhūmi, bhūm, q.v.

s.f. The earth, the world; earth, ground, soil, land, region, country (see bhū); place, site; estate, domain; room; receptacle (as of confidence, &c.); (in Geom.) base of a triangle, or other figure; (in Astron.) the symbol for one
subs: s.m. One who is invested by a proprietor with a portion of land which he must not dispose of
adj. & s.m. Born of, or on, the earth, earth-born; sprung from the ground; name of a low caste of Hindūs; the planet Mars; hell
s.m. The plant Kœmpferia rotunda; a kind of firework like the flower of that plant
adj. Burnt or reduced to earth (a corpse
such as bhūmi-pāl, bhūmi-jambu, bhūmi-ćāl, &c., see under the synonymous word bhū).

def: s.m. = bhūmiyā, q.v.
s.f. Preface (to a book), introduction.
s.m. Proprietor of the soil, landlord; an inhabitant long settled; village god or divinity; (in Myth.), An old snake with hair and whiskers
s.m. Native land, home (com. janam-bhūm).
s.m. Proprietor of the soil, landlord; an inhabitant long settled; village god or divinity; (in Myth.), An old snake with hair and whiskers
s.f. Ransacking, plundering, reducing to dust and ashes, laying waste. (The word points to a verbal form bhūmiyānā, from bhūmi).
s.m. One who lays waste, plunderer, ravager; insurgent or rebel chief.
s.f. A large kind of basket.
s.m. Landholder.
s.f. = bhūmi, q.v.
subs: bhauṅ-ćāl, bhūṅ-ḍol, s.m. Earthquake ( = bhū-ćāl, bhūmi-ćāl, &c. qq.v.)
bhūṅ-ḍarī, s.f. A small patch of cultivation allotted rent free to village servants
bhūṅ-gaʼī, s.f. A kind of tax on forest produce
(S. bhūmi+gr̤iha), s.m. Underground chamber, vault, cellar, cavern.

def: part. adj. (f. ī-), Roasted, broiled, grilled, parched

ety: v.n. from bhunānā, 'to get or have parched'
def: s.f. Price paid for parching grain.
s.f. Price paid for exchange (of money), discount or loss in exchange.
v.t. To cause to be changed; to change (money).

ety: caus. of bhunnā or bhūnnā, 'to parch
def: v.t. To cause to be fried or parched; to cause, or to get (one) to parch, &c.; to have or get (grain, &c.) parched or fried, &c.
adj. Unlucky, inauspicious
s.f. See s.v. bhūṅ.
s.m. One who cultivates with a borrowed plough or hand instrument.
s.f. A kind of worm covered with hair, the palmer-worm (cf. sūʼā-pokā).
s.m. Name of an agricultural tribe of Hindūs, or bastard Brāhmans, concerning whose origin many curious legends are current.
s.m. = bhoṅ-gharā, q.v.s.v. bhūṅ.

ety: Ap. Prk. भूविँआ, Prk. भूमिआ; S. भूमिका
def: s.f. = bhūṅ, bhūmi, qq.v.
s.m. = bhūmiyā, q.v.
s.m. Parcher of grain = bhaṛ-bhūṅjā, q.v.

ety: for bhujā, perf. part. of bhujnā = bhūnnā
def: s.m. Parched grain ( = bhujnā).
v.t. = bhognā, bhugatnā, qq.v.

ety: bhūṅk˚ = Prk. भुक्क(इ) = S. भक्ष्य(ति), future of rt. भष्
def: v.n. To bark (as a dog); to talk nonsensically or foolishly.
v.t. See بہننا bhunnā.
v.n. To be changed (money)
v.t. See بهوننا bhūnnā.

ety: bhūṅ˚ = Prk. भुणे(इ), or भुण(इ), fr. S. भूर्ण p.p.p. of rt. भ्रज्ज्
def: v.t. To roast, broil, fry, parch, scorch, burn; to consume, annihilate
subs: bhūn-ḍālnā, v.t. Intens. of, and = bhūnnā.

def: v.n. To be fried, roasted, grilled, parched; to roast or burn (mentally), to be in pain, to grieve; — v.t. To fry, parch, &c. = bhūnnā, q.v.
s.m. Strong post to which an elephant is tied ( = bhaunās); penis.
s.m. Chaff, &c. = bhus; bhūsā; qq.v.
subs: s.m. All kinds of grain in husk.

def: s.m. Chaff, &c. = bhus; bhūsā; qq.v.
subs: s.m. All kinds of grain in husk.

def: v.t. To make (a dog) bark.
s.f. = bhūsī, q.v.
v.n. To bark ( = bhauṅknā).
s.f. = bhunāʼī, q.v.
v.t. = bhunānā, q.v.
s.m. = bhūṅ-harā; bhoṅ-gharā.
s.m. See s.v. bhū.
s.f. Hole (through which water runs out); well, or gathering pit (dug by the brink of a stream, chiefly for irrigation: syn. bhurkī).
s.f. Spring, fountain, &c. = bhurkī, q.v.
s.f. Charity, almsgiving, alms; ( = būr); — bhūr bāṅṭnā, v.t. To give alms to a crowd of poor people.

ety: S. भूर्णि; or भूर; rt. भू
def: s.f. Sandy ground, porous soil; soil of the fourth quality (containing about seven tenths of sand, and the rest of clay).

ety: orig. bhūs, nom. sing. of bhū
def: adv. A mystical word (the first of the three Vyāhr̤itis, viz. bhūr, bhuvas, svar, uttered by every Brāhman in commencing his daily prayers); — s.f. The lowest of the seven worlds, the earth
adj. (f. -ī), Brown; auburn (hair).
s.m. = bahoṛā, q.v.
